description: university system in California, United States
description_wiki: The University of California (UC) is a public land-grant research university system in the U.S. state of California.

surface_forms: special legislation was then enacted in Sacramento
description: legislative branch of the state government of California
description_wiki: The California State Legislature is the bicameral state legislature of the U.S. state of California, consisting of the California State Assembly (lower house with 80 members) and the California State Senate (upper house with 40 members).

entity: Regents of the University of California
aliases: Regents of the University of California
University of California Board of Regents
The Regents of the University of California

THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA     (Oakland, CA)
description: governing board of the University of California
description_wiki: The Regents of the University of California (also referred to as the Board of Regents to distinguish the board from the corporation it governs of the same name) is the governing board of the University of California (UC), a public state university system in the U.S. state of California.

description: President of the University of California (1891-1975)
description_wiki: Robert Gordon Sproul ( SPROWL; May 22, 1891 – September 10, 1975) was the first systemwide president (1952–1958) of the University of California system, and the 11th president of the University of California, Berkeley, serving from 1930 to 1952.
World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: public research university in Minneapolis and Saint Paul, Minnesota, United States
description_wiki: The University of Minnesota Twin Cities (historically known as University of Minnesota) is a public land-grant research university in the Twin Cities of Minneapolis and Saint Paul, Minnesota, United States.

description: public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan
description_wiki: The University of Michigan (UMich, U-M, or Michigan) is a public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States.

description: public university in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: The Pennsylvania State University (Penn State or PSU) is a public state-related land-grant research university with campuses and facilities throughout Pennsylvania, United States.
The University of Virginia (UVA or Virginia) is a public research university located in Charlottesville City and Albemarle County in Virginia, United States.

A junior college is a type of post-secondary institution that offers vocational and academic training that is designed to prepare students for either skilled trades and technical occupations or support roles in professions such as engineering, accountancy, business administration, nursing, medicine, architecture, and criminology.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: state legislature of the U.S. state of Wisconsin
description_wiki: The Wisconsin State Legislature is the state legislature of the U.S. state of Wisconsin.
